assertions.title: MissionPage toggles between Chat and Mission Control without changing sessions

steps.action: Open the existing Mission session named similar to 'foundation-metadata mission identity val...' in the Mission-GUI-TEST project
steps.expected: MissionPage is active with the Mission view toggle visible
steps.observed: MissionPage rendered with data-testid='mission-view-toggle' and the active session remained mission session a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391

steps.action: Toggle from Chat to Mission Control using the MissionPage view toggle
steps.expected: Mission Control renders without changing the active orchestrator session
steps.observed: Mission Control content became visible, feature queue content rendered, and the active session id stayed a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391

steps.action: Toggle back from Mission Control to Chat
steps.expected: Chat view returns while keeping the same Mission session active
steps.observed: Chat view restored with AskUser and Tasks shell elements visible, and the active session id still matched a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391

evidence.consoleErrors: React emitted duplicate-key warnings for mission handoff entries: mission-handoff-mission-session-entry-routing-and-sidebar and mission-handoff-scrutiny-validator-mission-sync-recovery.
evidence.network: No requests captured by agent-browser during the toggle flow; the Electron renderer appeared to switch views from existing in-app state.

assertions.title: Mission Chat view preserves the existing conversation shell

steps.action: Inspect the Mission Chat view in the existing Mission session
steps.expected: The Mission Chat view keeps the normal conversation shell behaviors while remaining on the Mission session
steps.observed: Chat view stayed on mission session a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391 and showed normal shell controls including AskUser, Tasks 3/5, model/autonomy selectors, and the disabled mission-running input

steps.action: Check for visible shell elements called out by the contract
steps.expected: At least the visible ask-user/todo/debug or permission-related shell surfaces remain available if present
steps.observed: AskUser and Tasks were visible in Chat view; no permission card or debug trace panel was present in this session state

steps.action: Confirm the Mission status bar while remaining in Chat view
steps.expected: Mission status bar remains visible without changing sessions
steps.observed: The bottom mission status bar remained visible in Chat view with state, progress, current feature, worker, and update text while the active session id stayed unchanged

evidence.consoleErrors: React emitted duplicate-key warnings for mission handoff entries: mission-handoff-mission-session-entry-routing-and-sidebar and mission-handoff-scrutiny-validator-mission-sync-recovery.
evidence.network: No requests captured by agent-browser while validating Chat shell preservation in the Electron Mission view.

assertions.title: Mission status bar remains visible in both Chat and Mission Control views

steps.action: Inspect the Mission page in Chat view
steps.expected: A bottom status bar with data-testid='mission-statusbar' is visible in Chat view
steps.observed: Chat view showed the mission status bar with state Running, progress 10/13, current feature text, worker c6da10ce…aaa2, and update text 'Mission is running'

steps.action: Toggle to Mission Control and inspect the bottom bar again
steps.expected: The same mission status bar remains visible in Mission Control view
steps.observed: Mission Control view still showed data-testid='mission-statusbar' with the same mission state, progress, current feature, worker, and update context

evidence.consoleErrors: React emitted duplicate-key warnings for mission handoff entries: mission-handoff-mission-session-entry-routing-and-sidebar and mission-handoff-scrutiny-validator-mission-sync-recovery.
evidence.network: No requests captured by agent-browser while validating status bar persistence across Mission views.

summary: Tested 3 assertions in the existing Mission-GUI-TEST Mission session. VAL-ROUTING-003, VAL-ROUTING-006, and VAL-CONTROL-011 passed. The same Mission session stayed active across Chat and Mission Control toggles, the Chat shell preserved visible AskUser/Tasks surfaces, and the mission status bar remained visible in both views. React duplicate-key console warnings were present during the run but did not block the asserted behaviors.
